description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rough, angular exterior and a flat, freshly cut top surface. The stone's body is predominantly a warm, earthy brown with visible textural variations and some lighter beige areas, especially on the cut face. One side of the stone features a distinct fossil imprint, likely of a trilobite, adding a unique and visually striking detail. The surfaces are uneven, with jagged edges and natural fractures, suggesting it was broken from a larger rock. The fossil side is smoother and more visually interesting, while the other sides are more rugged and coarse. The stone appears dense and solid, typical of sedimentary rocks found in outdoor urban or park environments.
description: Irregular brown stone with a flat cut top and a prominent fossil imprint on one side. The surface is rough and angular, with natural fractures and earthy color variations.
description_view_0: A long, angular side of the stone is visible, showing the rough brown texture and the flat, pale cut top.
description_view_1: The broadest face is shown, highlighting the flat, freshly cut top and the rugged, blocky brown surface.
description_view_2: A narrow, jagged edge is visible, with the fossil imprint just starting to come into view along the side.
description_view_3: The fossil-bearing face is fully visible, displaying the detailed trilobite imprint and a smoother, flatter surface compared to other sides.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or decorative obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Distinct fossil imprint on one face, with irregular natural fractures and subtle color mottling throughout the stone.
attribute_weathering: Rough with some freshly cut surfaces and minor natural erosion, indicating partial exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, but can blend with other natural stones or garden features.
